name: Build Response

label: Credito - Registrar Utilizacao
info:
POST /orcamentos/creditos/registrar-utilizacao

Body: { token, orcamentoid, creditoid, valor }
Token is accepted but ignored (CF Access handles auth externally).

Logic:
1. Validate valor > 0 and UUIDs
2. Load credito; verify valor <= valor_disponivel
3. Load orcamento to get cliente_id and numero_da_proposta
4. Create utilizacoes_de_credito row (utilizacao_firmada=false, tipo_de_utilizacao=100000000)
5. Update credito.valor_utilizado = +valor (valor_disponivel is generated)

Response: { ok: true, utilizacaoId, valorUtilizado, valorDisponivel }

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute Registrar Utilizacao
Error Handler

label: Credito - Cancelar Utilizacao
info:
POST /orcamentos/creditos/cancelar-utilizacao

Body: { token, id } (id = utilizacao uuid)

Logic (from PA `(Crédito do Cliente) - Cancelar Utilização de Crédito`):
1. Load utilizacao
2. If already utilizacao_firmada=true -> no-op (aligned with PA behavior)
3. Mark utilizacao_cancelada=true
4. Decrement credito.valor_utilizado by utilizacao.valor_utilizado

Response: { ok: true, id, cancelled: boolean, reason? }

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute Cancel
Error Handler

label: Credito - Firmar Utilizacoes
info:
POST /orcamentos/creditos/firmar-utilizacoes

Body: { token, orcamentoId } (also accepts orcamentoid alias)

Also callable internally via link-in node 'firmar-utilizacoes-internal' (msg.orcamentoId set by caller).
Used by publicar-pedido (Batch 6).

Logic (from PA `(Crédito do Cliente) - Firmar Utilizações de um orçamento`):
1. GET utilizacoes_de_credito for orcamento where utilizacao_cancelada != true
2. For each: PATCH utilizacao_firmada=true

Response: { ok: true, orcamentoId, count }

name: Validate HTTP Input
Env Guard (HTTP only)
Validate Internal Input
Execute Firmar
Error Handler

label: Credito - Registrar Reembolso
info:
POST /orcamentos/creditos/registrar-reembolso

Body: { token, creditoid, valor }

Logic (from PA `(Crédito do Cliente) - Registrar Reembolso de Crédito`):
1. Validate valor > 0
2. Load credito; verify valor <= valor_disponivel
3. Create utilizacoes_de_credito: tipo_de_utilizacao=100000001 (reembolso), utilizacao_firmada=true, orcamento_id=null
4. Update credito.valor_utilizado += valor

Response: { ok: true, utilizacaoId, valorUtilizado, valorDisponivel }

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute Reembolso
Error Handler

label: Credito - Gerar de Devolucao OS
info:
POST /orcamentos/creditos/gerar-de-devolucao-os

Body: { token, prodOsId, itemOrcamentoId, prodOsQuantidade }

Since produtos_servico in Supabase is missing opcao_de_devolucao and item_de_orcamento_id columns, the caller MUST pass prodOsId (identifier for bookkeeping only) and prodOsQuantidade (read from Dataverse prodos.quantidade upstream).

Logic (from PA `(Crédito do Cliente) - Gerar Crédito de devolução de OS`):
1. GET itens_de_orcamento[itemOrcamentoId] for quantidade_disponivel_devolucao, valor_devolucao_disponivel, orcamento_id
2. GET orcamentos[orcamento_id] -> cliente_id
3. Branch:
   - If quantidade_disponivel_devolucao >= prodOsQuantidade -> credit of full valor_devolucao_disponivel
   - Else -> credit of (valor_devolucao_disponivel / quantidade_disponivel_devolucao) * prodOsQuantidade
4. Create creditos_do_cliente row
5. Update item.quantidade_devolvida += prodOsQuantidade
6. NOTE: PA also updates prodos.opcao_de_devolucao=100000002; Supabase produtos_servico lacks this column. Caller must update Dataverse separately.

Response: { ok: true, creditoId, valorDoCredito, branch, produtosServicoUpdated: false, note }

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute Gerar Devolucao OS
Error Handler

label: Item - Inserir Sobreposicao
info:
POST /orcamentos/itens/on-create

Body: { token, itemid }

Per PA '(Item de Orçamento) - Quando um item é criado, inserir valor de sobreposição vigente':
Loads item; if valor_de_sobreposicao OR valor_servico_de_sobreposicao is null/0/negative AND produto_id set:
  Fetches precos_de_produto.{preco_de_venda, preco_servico_instalacao}
  If preco_de_venda > 0, patches item with both sobreposicao values.
Returns { ok, applied, itemId, reason? }.

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute
Error Handler

label: Desmembrar Kits
info:
POST /orcamentos/desmembrar-kits

Body: { token, orcamentoid }

Per PA '(Orçamento) - Desmembrar Kits':
Phase A: for each kit item (opcao_de_fornecimento=100000000, kit=true, state_code=0, removido!=true):
  list itens_de_kit children; for each, create a non-kit item with qty = parent.qty * child.qty.
  Delete original kit item.
Phase B: group remaining items by (produto_id, section, ambiente); for groups with >=2 items,
  create one consolidated item with summed qty, then delete duplicates.
Returns counts for both phases.

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute
Error Handler

label: Pedidos PDF - Sync (core)
info:
POST /orcamentos/pedidos-pdf/sync

Body: {spItem} — single SharePoint item payload (all fields optional).

PLACEHOLDER implementation. PA '(Pedidos em PDF) - Sharepoint→Dataverse dataflow' refreshes a Power Automate Dataflow (id ef45bbfa-f86a-4b94-9fd8-46f6c3c22ea9) that maps SharePoint list 'Novos Pedidos' in /sites/vendas to Dataverse cr22f_uniumpedidoempdfs via Power Query M scripts. This endpoint is the core sync subflow and is HTTP-callable by /sync-manual and /on-upload. When the M-script mapping is migrated to Node-RED + Graph API, this flow should: (1) receive a single SP list item payload (fields + id + url); (2) lookup existence in public.pedidos_pdf by nome_do_arquivo+link_do_arquivo; (3) POST (create) or PATCH (update) accordingly; (4) return {ok:true, action, pedidoPdfId}. Requires Graph API app credentials (not yet provisioned on orcamentos container).

PA '(Pedidos em PDF) - Quando uma postagem e criada em novos pedidos, engatilhar dataflow' subscribes to Teams channel messages on group 1d638f3d-d39c-4b9a-b2bc-b5df8460f550 channel 19:29046801417c4c989b380920848c0b2e@thread.tacv2 and refreshes the same PA Dataflow. When migrated, this endpoint should: (a) handle SP webhook validation handshake (already implemented); (b) for each change-notification entry, invoke the core sync subflow. label: PDF - Upload Temp
info:
POST /orcamentos/pdf/upload-temp

Body: { file: { name: string, contentBytes: string (base64) } } (PA-compatible)
Alternate: { file_name, file_base64 }

Logic:
1. Validate: file name + base64 (max 20MB decoded), .pdf extension
2. Env Guard (dev→403 unless prod)
3. Sanitize filename (strip control chars, illegal chars); prepend 'pedido-' + UTC timestamp for safe cleanup targeting and collision avoidance
4. PUT object to MinIO bucket 'pedidos' via AWS4 signature (+ CF Access headers)
5. INSERT s3_objects row with {bucket_name: 'pedidos', object_key} (remote_url and local_url are GENERATED)
6. Response: { ok: true, url, filepath, key, s3_objects_id }

Notes:
- The 'pedido-' filename prefix is critical for the pdf-cleanup-cache flow — it lets cleanup scope to our orcamento PDF cache only, never touching other rows in the shared 'pedidos' bucket (e.g., comprovantes, task attachments).
- PA '(PDF) - Upload Temporary Orçamento PDF File' (workflowid 10b5ff17-549c-ed11-aad1-000d3ac190e8) uploaded to SharePoint /Shared Documents/Cache/Orçamentos and created an anonymous sharing link. Here the file goes to MinIO 'pedidos' and the returned 'url' is the MinIO public URL (signed via bucket policy — see nodered-storage DeletarArquivo behavior).
- The 'Compress PDF' Adobe step from PA is skipped (not installed). The limit is 20MB decoded.

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Upload to MinIO + Insert s3_objects
Error Handler

label: PDF - Merge Temp (placeholder)
info:
POST /orcamentos/pdf/merge-temp

PLACEHOLDER.

Body: { filesPathList: string (comma-separated SP paths) } (PA compat) or { files: [{ name, base64 }] }

PA '(PDF) - Merge Temporary Orçamento PDFs' (workflowid e1f128f5-16af-ee11-a568-00224837ff5e) uses:
  - Microsoft SharePoint 'Get file content by path' to fetch each PDF
  - Plumsail 'FlowV1DocumentsJobsMergePdfPost' to merge PDFs
  - Child flow 'Upload Temporary PDF' to upload the merged PDF back to SP

None of these PA connectors have a Node-RED equivalent installed. To implement real merging we need either:
  (a) install 'pdf-lib' (Node.js npm package) in this container (functionLocalModules or package.json) and merge in-process
  (b) use a separate service (Plumsail proxy, PDF-renderer worker)
Both are out of scope for Batch 4. This endpoint returns a placeholder ack so callers do not break; integration tests must assert the `merged: false` flag and `reason` string.

Follow-up env/deps for a real implementation:
  - pdf-lib (npm): for in-process merge. Roughly: load each file → copyPages → save
  - Or: provision a Plumsail / PDF microservice and forward the base64 array to it

Response: { ok: true, merged: false, reason: 'pdf-merge-library-not-installed-dev-placeholder', filesCount }

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute (placeholder)
Error Handler

  1. Child flow 'Merge Temporary Orçamento PDFs' (placeholder above — blocks on Plumsail)
  2. Send email via 'Send mail notification (V3)' (connector shared_sendmail)

Dependencies missing in Node-RED:

  - Email sender. Options: (a) Office365 via Graph (requires DATAVERSE_TENANT_ID/CLIENT_ID/CLIENT_SECRET — present on storage but not on this module yet); (b) SMTP via node-red-contrib-smtp; (c) SendGrid / Postmark API.

Follow-up env/creds for a real implementation:
  - GRAPH creds (tenant/client/secret) OR SMTP creds (host/port/user/pass)
  - Sender email address for 'from' field
  - PDF merge library (pdf-lib, etc.)

Response: { ok: true, merged: false, sent: false, reason: 'pdf-merge-and-email-not-implemented-dev-placeholder', filesCount, to }

Dependencies missing in Node-RED:

  - The Epson printer address should be configurable per environment (env var EPSON_PRINT_EMAIL).

Follow-up env/creds for a real implementation:

  - Graph OR SMTP creds for sending

label: PDF - Cleanup Cache (scheduled)
info:
Scheduled + manual: POST /orcamentos/pdf/cleanup-cache

Trigger: inject timer every 24h (disabled in DEV via Env Guard) + HTTP POST (manual, Env Guard blocks dev).

Logic:
1. Compute threshold = now - 30 days.
2. GET /rest/v1/s3_objects?bucket_name=eq.pedidos&object_key=like.pedido-*&created_at=lt.{threshold}&select=id,object_key,bucket_name
   (Scope restricted to orcamento PDF cache only — object_key prefix 'pedido-', set by pdf-upload-temp)
3. For each row: DELETE MinIO object (AWS4) then DELETE /rest/v1/s3_objects?id=eq.{uuid}.
4. Response: { ok: true, expired_found, deleted_minio, deleted_rows, failures[] }

Safety:
- Only affects rows whose object_key starts with 'pedido-' — the prefix our pdf-upload-temp flow applies. This avoids touching comprovantes (prefix 'comprovante_'), task attachments, call recordings, etc.
- PostgREST DELETE on s3_objects failing with HTTP 409 (FK conflict) does NOT block the scheduled run; failures are surfaced in the response.

Notes:
- PA '(PDF) - Delete expired cache files' (workflowid f2ea4d99-7f64-ef11-a670-002248df332d) queried Dataverse 'new_expiredcachefiles' + deleted SharePoint files + refreshed a Dataflow. Here we target MinIO 's3_objects' table with bucket 'pedidos' + object_key LIKE 'pedido-%'. Dataflow refresh not applicable — orcamentos domain uses live DB reads.
- Body { maxAge: days } accepted (default 30) for manual runs; default stays 30 for the scheduled trigger.
- Env Guard still applies; in prod the daily inject triggers the cleanup, in dev only manual calls with prod env would run.

name: Validate Input
Env Guard
Execute Cleanup
Error Handler

label: Clicksign - Enviar Comprovante
Clicksign - Core Envelope (subflow)
Clicksign - Webhook
info:
POST /orcamentos/clicksign/webhook
Receives Clicksign callbacks. Events:
  - 'sign': marks signer as signed (updates comprovantes_de_pagamento.assinado + data_da_assinatura, or impressoes_de_recibo.signed_on_datetime + situacao=538780001)
  - 'document_closed': downloads signed PDF, uploads to MinIO, records s3_objects, updates comprovantes_de_pagamento.arquivo_s3_id

Returns 200 quickly to ACK the webhook.
